columns.name: Id
columns.description: Unique identifier of the actionable alert

columns.name: AlertDate
columns.description: Date and time when the alert was generated

columns.name: AlertTitle
columns.description: Title of the actionable alert

columns.name: AlertName
columns.description: Name of the actionable alert as returned by the Cybersixgill API

columns.name: AlertTypeId
columns.description: Alert type identifier

columns.name: Content
columns.description: Content details of the actionable alert. Matches the Azure Function branching: when additional_info.cve_id is present, use additional_info.cve_description; otherwise fall back to es_item.content when the top-level content field is empty.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Severity level of the alert as an integer score (e.g. 10)

columns.name: ThreatLevel
columns.description: Threat level of the alert (e.g. imminent, emerging)

columns.name: Threats
columns.description: Threats associated with the alert, serialized from array

columns.name: EsId
columns.description: Elasticsearch document identifier for the underlying intelligence item

columns.name: UserId
columns.description: User ID associated with the alert

columns.name: AlertCreationDate
columns.description: Date and time when the alert record was ingested

columns.name: Time
columns.description: Timestamp of the alert, mirrored from the date field

columns.name: Read
columns.description: Indicates whether the alert has been read

columns.name: Site
columns.description: Site where the threat was observed

columns.name: Assets
columns.description: Flat list of asset values (bins + domain_names) concatenated from matched_assets, mirroring the Azure Function flattening behaviour.

columns.name: MatchedAssetsBins
columns.description: Matched assets bins extracted from matched_assets

columns.name: MatchedAssetsDomainNames
columns.description: Domain names extracted from matched_assets

columns.name: SubAlertsCount
columns.description: Number of sub-alerts. Computed from array_length(sub_alerts) to match the Azure Function (which uses len(sub_alerts) regardless of the API-supplied sub_alerts_count).

columns.name: SubAlerts
columns.description: Raw sub_alerts array from the Cybersixgill API, preserved as a dynamic column. The CyberSixgillAlertsExpanded parser uses mv-expand on this column at query time to fan out one row per sub-alert - matching the per-sub-alert rows the legacy Azure Function emitted into CyberSixgill_Alerts_CL.

columns.name: StatusName
columns.description: Machine-readable status name of the alert (e.g. treatment_required). Defaults to 'Treatment Required' when missing, matching the Azure Function fallback behaviour.

columns.name: ThreatActor
columns.description: Threat actor (dark web username or channel name) extracted from es_item

columns.name: ThreatSource
columns.description: Dark web site or platform where the threat was observed, extracted from es_item

columns.name: PortalUrl
columns.description: Direct link to this alert in the Cybersixgill portal

columns.name: Cve
columns.description: CVE identifier when the alert relates to a vulnerability. Empty string for non-CVE alerts. Mirrors the Azure Function cve_s field, sourced from additional_info.cve_id in the per-alert detail response.

columns.name: CveUrl
columns.description: Direct link to the CVE record in the Cybersixgill portal when cve_id is present. Empty string otherwise. Mirrors the Azure Function cve_url_s field.

columns.name: CyberSixgillCvss31
columns.description: CVSS v3.1 score for CVE-related alerts, sourced from additional_info.nvd.v3.current. Defaults to -1 when the CVE has no v3 score (matches Azure Function); null for non-CVE alerts.

columns.name: CyberSixgillCvss20
columns.description: CVSS v2.0 score for CVE-related alerts, sourced from additional_info.nvd.v2.current. Defaults to -1 when the CVE has no v2 score (matches Azure Function); null for non-CVE alerts.

columns.name: CyberSixgillDveScore
columns.description: Cybersixgill DVE (Dynamic Vulnerability Exploit) score for CVE-related alerts, sourced from additional_info.score.current. Null for non-CVE alerts.

columns.name: OrganizationName
columns.description: Name of the organization the alert was raised for, sourced from additional_info.organization_name. Mirrors the Azure Function field of the same name.

columns.name: ActorUrlWithContext
columns.description: Direct link to the threat actor in the Cybersixgill portal, scoped by source site. Empty string when threat_actor is unknown. Mirrors the Azure Function actor_url_with_context_s, computed from es_item.creator and es_item.site (the AzFunc populates this only when its 3rd-call branch sets threat_actor; CCF computes it whenever es_item.creator is present, which is strictly more coverage).

columns.name: ActorUrlWithoutContext
columns.description: Direct link to the threat actor in the Cybersixgill portal, without source-site scope. Empty string when threat_actor is unknown. Mirrors the Azure Function actor_url_without_context_s.

columns.name: AdditionalInfo
columns.description: Nested additional_info object from the per-alert detail call. Declared so DCR transforms can extract cve_id, nvd.v3/v2.current, score.current, organization_name.

columns.name: EsItem
columns.description: Nested es_item object from the per-alert detail call. Declared so DCR transforms can extract creator, site, content.

columns.name: MatchedAssets
columns.description: Nested matched_assets object from the bulk alert response. Declared so DCR transforms can extract bins and domain_names.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Nested status object from the bulk alert response. Declared so DCR transforms can extract status.name with the 'Treatment Required' fallback.

columns.name: SourceSystem
columns.description: Legacy HTTP Data Collector field. Hard-coded to 'RestAPI' to preserve byte-compatibility with the Azure Function table so production parsers behave identically across AzFunc and CCF ingestion paths.

columns.name: MG
columns.description: Legacy SCOM Management Group GUID. Populated with the empty-GUID sentinel to preserve byte-compatibility with the Azure Function table - there is no real management group in the CCF/DCR pipeline.

columns.name: ManagementGroupName
columns.description: Legacy SCOM Management Group name. Populated with empty string to preserve byte-compatibility with the Azure Function table - SCOM is not part of the CCF/DCR pipeline.

columns.name: Computer
columns.description: Legacy HTTP Data Collector auto-field carrying the source host name. Populated with empty string to preserve byte-compatibility with the Azure Function table - ingestion is direct HTTPS from Cybersixgill so there is no source host.
